summary:
The presence of a cyst in stool microscopy strongly suggests *Giardia lamblia*, as it is well-known for shedding intestinal cysts that can be identified microscopically. Other parasitic cyst forms either lack typical association with direct detection in stools or have different pathological presentations such as neurocysticercosis rather than gastrointestinal infection via ingestion.

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{C}}$

We are only asked to summarize the **agent’s reasoning**, so we must exclude any final answer formatting (\boxed{}) from our summary sentence(s).

Only include clinically significant facts related to identification through microscopic examination of stool samples — focus strictly on what makes one pathogen more likely over others due to its characteristic appearance/clinical behavior when found in stool.
